vectors.name: Int Function
vectors.description: Returns the integer part of a real number.

quirks_and_tests.description: The Int() function returns a REAL type, not an integer. It simply truncates the decimal part without rounding.

vectors.name: Trunc Function
vectors.description: Truncates a real-type value to an integer-type value.

quirks_and_tests.description: It removes the fractional part, rounding toward zero.

vectors.name: Round Function
vectors.description: Rounds a real-type value to the nearest integer. If the value is exactly halfway between two integers, it rounds to the number with the greatest absolute magnitude (away from zero).

quirks_and_tests.description: Turbo Pascal employs standard arithmetic rounding (rounding away from zero). A fractional part of exactly .5 will always be rounded to the number with the greatest absolute magnitude, meaning positive numbers round up and negative numbers round down.

vectors.name: Integer Division (DIV)
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Longint DIV Operand2: Longint; { Returns Longint }
vectors.description: Performs integer division. Truncates the result towards zero.

quirks_and_tests.description: Unlike standard real division (/), DIV strictly returns an integer.

vectors.name: Modulo Operator (MOD)
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Longint MOD Operand2: Longint; { Returns Longint }
vectors.description: Returns the remainder of integer division.

quirks_and_tests.description: Standard modulo arithmetic. The sign of the result depends on the dividend.
